title: CRWD unwind — staged diversification of the vested employer RSUs
description: 210 vested shares of the employer stock is the single largest position — roughly 40% of the taxable account riding one name AND the paycheck. Unwind in staged tranches: oldest long-term gain lots first, never inside a trading blackout, proceeds to the growth core. The loss lots stay back as tax-harvest inventory. Children carry the mechanical steps.

title: Tranche 1 — sell the 2024-08 deep-gain lot (60 sh, limit 378)
description: The oldest lot, long-term, deepest gain — the cheapest tax exit and the biggest concentration cut per share sold. Ticket written 6/17; the resting limit waits for the trading window.
close_reason: Filled at $378.20 on 7/11 after the trading window reopened — the oldest long-term lot is out. Proceeds (~$22.7k) redeployed into QQQ the same session per the 6/17 execution ticket; single-employer weight drops from ~40% to ~31% of taxable.

comments.author: quant
comments.text: Fill confirmed against the account history; the 6/17 execution ticket is fully executed. Remaining CRWD basis is the 2025-02 gain lot plus the 2025-08 / 2026-02 loss lots.

title: Confirm the trading window stays open for the tranche-2 sale
description: The employer trading policy and the earnings calendar decide this, not the market: the window that opened 7/9 typically closes two weeks ahead of the quarterly print. Pin down the exact close date before any tranche-2 order rests — a GTC order that outlives the window is a policy violation, not a trade.

title: Place the tranche-2 limit sale — the 2025-02 lot (55 sh)
description: Next-oldest lot, long-term as of this February. Same playbook as tranche 1: a limit above the 20-day range, good only while the window is open, proceeds to the core. Blocked until scale-u3 pins the window's close date — the order must expire before the blackout starts.

title: Harvest the 2026-02 loss lot before the vest poisons the window
description: The 45-share February lot sits under water — a clean tax-loss harvest, but only if it executes before the 8/15 vest opens a wash window. Per scale-v4's map the poison zone runs 7/16 through 9/14: clear the sale by 7/15 or park this until mid-September.

title: Vest planning — the 8/15 tranche lands prepared, not improvised
description: The vest calendar runs quarterly: 45 units on 8/15, again 11/15 and 2/15. Every vest is a taxable event, a withholding gap, and a wash-sale landmine for the loss lots. This epic does the math and the decisions ahead of the date instead of the week after.

title: Model the withholding shortfall on the 8/15 vest
description: RSU vests withhold at the flat supplemental rate, which runs well under the actual marginal rate — the gap compounds quietly across four vests a year and surfaces as an April surprise.
close_reason: Modeled: the default 22% supplemental withholding runs ~11 points under the marginal rate — roughly a $1.9k gap on a 45-unit vest at current prices. Plan: cover it with the 9/15 1040-ES Q3 payment rather than touching payroll elections.

title: Decide sell-to-cover vs hold for the 8/15 tranche
description: The standing plan treats new vests as diversification supply, not conviction adds — the default is a day-of-vest sale of the full tranche (no gain, no wash risk, no new concentration). Confirm against the unwind pace once the tranche-2 outcome is known.

title: Map the wash-sale windows around the vest calendar
description: A vest is an acquisition — each one poisons a loss sale of the same stock for 30 days on either side. With quarterly vests, the safe harvest windows are narrower than they look; map them once so every loss-lot decision reads off the same calendar.
close_reason: Mapped: the 8/15 vest poisons any CRWD loss sale from 7/16 through 9/14; the 11/15 vest repeats the pattern 10/16 through 12/15. Loss harvests must clear by 7/15 or wait for the mid-September gap. Map posted to the unwind dashboard.

comments.author: quant
comments.text: Cross-checked against the vest_calendar in portfolio.yaml — all three upcoming vests inherit the same ±30-day poison window. scale-u6 is the only task racing the 7/15 cutoff.

title: Diversification base — build the boring layer under the aggressive book
description: The book is all growth: a thin cash buffer, no bonds, and one employer name at a third of taxable. This epic builds the floor — an emergency fund at six months, overlap-aware core adds — so the concentrated bets sit on something solid instead of on nerve.

title: Raise the emergency-fund auto-transfer toward the 6-month target
description: The $13k buffer covers under four months of spend — thin for a book this volatile plus a job search in flight. Raise the monthly auto-transfer and let tranche proceeds top it up opportunistically.
close_reason: Auto-transfer raised from $400 to $700/mo into the high-yield savings. At that rate the $13k balance reaches the ~$21k six-month target in roughly 11 months — faster if a tranche sale tops it up along the way.

comments.author: scout
comments.text: Rate check done: the current HYSA APY sits within 0.2% of the best broadly available rate — not worth a bank move for the spread.

title: Overlap check — SMH vs QQQ before any new core money
description: Before the next contribution picks a destination, quantify how much of SMH already lives inside QQQ — the book may be holding the same semiconductor bet twice and calling it diversification. Output: an overlap table plus a recommendation for where new core money goes.

title: Assemble the HashiCorp onsite prep pack
description: The onsite loop is scheduled — systems design plus two behavioral rounds. Pack contents: an IaC-heavy systems-design dry run, STAR stories mapped to the posted competencies, and comp anchors from the posted $168k–$216k band before any number gets said out loud.

title: Audit brokerage account security — 2FA, alerts, withdrawal lock
description: One hour of hardening on the account that holds most of the net worth: hardware-key 2FA, transfer alerts on, a withdrawal lock on the sweep. Cheap insurance, no market opinion required.

title: Revisit the ARM watchlist idea after the late-August print
description: Parked deliberately: no new thematic adds while the unwind is mid-flight. Re-read the thesis after the earnings print, and only act if the diversification base is on track — the watchlist note says candidate, not commitment.

title: Fare watch — Denver to Portland under $180 for the September weekend
description: The Portland long weekend (9/4–9/7) is in planning and fares hover just above the target. Book on a dip; no checked bags, no seat-selection upsell — the budget-trip discipline is the point.
